description:
You are helping a script-developer who maintains small command-line utilities for the logistics team.  
The developer has just dropped two files into the data directory:

1. /home/user/data/inventory.json – current warehouse stock
2. /home/user/data/inventory_schema.json – a very small JSON-Schema description of the expected structure

Goal  
Write ONE single, chained shell command (a single line on one prompt; you may use &&, ||, pipes, subshells, etc.) that does both of the following things in order:

A) Validation step  
   • Use jq only (no external Python/Node packages) to make sure /home/user/data/inventory.json  
     satisfies all of these conditions at once:  
       – it is a JSON object containing keys “warehouse” and “items”.  
       – .warehouse is a string.  
       – .items is an array of objects, and every object inside it has integer “id”, string “name”,  
         integer “quantity”, and the “quantity” value is ≥ 0.  
   • The command must exit with status 0 when (and only when) every one of those assertions is true.  
   • If any assertion fails the command must exit with a non-zero status and must NOT continue to part (B).

B) Report-generation step  
   • When and only when part (A) succeeds, create the directory /home/user/report if it does not yet exist  
     and write a new file called /home/user/report/low_stock_report.json.  
   • The file content must be a compact (minified) JSON array that lists, in the order they appear in
     inventory.json, the id numbers of every product whose quantity is strictly less than 10.  
   • The file must contain exactly one trailing newline character and nothing else (no spaces, no pretty printing).  

Format that the automatic tests will look for in /home/user/report/low_stock_report.json:
Example (do **not** hard-code; the ids depend on the data):
[101,203]
↑ one newline here, then end of file.

You may assume:
• The jq utility is available in $PATH.
• You do NOT have root privileges.
• No other special utilities are guaranteed to exist.
• The two data files mentioned above already exist and are readable.

Success criteria checked by the grader:
1. Exit status of the chained command is 0.
2. /home/user/report/low_stock_report.json exists.
3. The JSON inside that file matches the items with quantity < 10 from inventory.json,
   is minified, and has exactly one newline at EOF.
name: task_000000_38c1975a
